criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/capitalize.sh defines a function capitalize that prints its first argument with the first character uppercased (ASCII), rest unchanged.
criteria.evidence: Diff adds scripts/lib/capitalize.sh lines 3-8: capitalize() uppercases "${value:0:1}" via LC_ALL=C tr 'a-z' 'A-Z' (ASCII-only) and prints "${value:1}" unchanged; ${1-} guards missing arg under set -u.
tests/capitalize.sh line 20 asserts assert_capitalize 'hello world' 'Hello world'; orchestrator evidence: bash tests/capitalize.sh exited 0.
tests/capitalize.sh line 21 asserts assert_capitalize '' ''; with value='', both ${value:0:1} and ${value:1} are empty so only the trailing newline is emitted (stripped by $(...)); test exited 0.
tests/capitalize.sh line 22 asserts assert_capitalize '9lives' '9lives'; tr 'a-z' 'A-Z' leaves '9' unchanged; test exited 0.

criteria.criterion: tests/capitalize.sh sources the helper, asserts those cases, and exits 0 when all pass.
criteria.evidence: tests/capitalize.sh line 5 `source scripts/lib/capitalize.sh`; lines 20-22 assert all three cases; set -euo pipefail (line 2) makes any assert_capitalize failure (return 1) abort nonzero; orchestrator evidence shows exit 0.

scope_finding: Only scripts/lib/capitalize.sh and tests/capitalize.sh are touched — both within in_scope globs (scripts/lib/**, tests/capitalize.sh); no out-of-scope paths (.github/**, specs/**, .orchestrator/**) modified. Orchestrator scope check: PASS.
regression_finding: Both files are new; no existing code is modified, so no regression surface. Minor note (non-blocking): capitalize emits a trailing newline via printf '%s\n', which is conventional shell output behavior and is stripped by command substitution in consumers; none observed as a defect.
security_findings: None observed. No secrets, no network use (needs_network: false honored), no credential access; shell usage is safe — variables are quoted, LC_ALL=C tr/printf only, and the test uses set -euo pipefail with quoted expansions.
reasons: All five acceptance criteria are satisfied by the new scripts/lib/capitalize.sh and tests/capitalize.sh, with orchestrator-verified test_command exit 0.
Changed paths are strictly within the approved scope and orchestrator integrity/scope checks both PASS.
No security concerns: no network, secrets, or unsafe shell constructs; expansions are quoted and the ASCII-only tr translation matches the spec's ASCII requirement.
